rationale: Subject home identified via orange bounding box and reticle. Bounding box measured at ~278 px (W) × ~337 px (H) = 54.3 ft × 65.9 ft = 3,578 sq ft bounding area. Roof is a complex multi-section all-hip design with a main central body, several projecting wings, and an attached garage/addition at the south. No gable ends visible, so rake = 0. Applied an 82% fill factor (dense hip coverage in plan view) to get ~2,940 sq ft footprint. Three ridge segments identified at peak of each major section totaling ~35 ft. Approximately 11 hip lines (sloped corner edges) averaging ~62 px each = ~133 ft. Five valley lines at inward intersections of roof planes = ~51 ft. Eave perimeter traced around all lower roof edges including wing projections = ~264 ft. Confidence reduced to 0.53 due to tree canopy obscuring portions of the south and west eave lines, and complexity of distinguishing individual hip vs. valley lines in the multi-section design.
